text.title: Client Trust Rebuild Comms Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Client Trust Rebuild Comms Templates application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apology email and reset deck contain no excuses or external-blame phrasing. Drafts containing 'because of X external party' = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Excuses extend the trust gap; ownership closes it.

rule.statement: Every commitment names a deliverable, a date, and an owner. Vague 'we will improve' = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Vague commitments are how rescue cycles fail again.

rule.statement: Comms artefacts name a single PM owner and a single client counterpart. No team-blanket sender.
Commitments with financial implications carry a finance sign-off note. Unsigned commitments = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Comms-driven scope creep is how rescues lose money.

rule.statement: Comms artefacts carry semver + date. Prior versions append-only in the rescue file.
rule.rationale: Comms versions are the trust-rebuild evidence.

rule.statement: Recovery digest sends weekly without gaps. Missed week = trust regression; flag as P0.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
